citeinfo.origin: Department of Commerce (DOC), National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA), National Ocean Service (NOS), Office for Coastal Management (OCM)
citeinfo.title: NOAA C-CAP National Wetland Potential

pubinfo.publish: NOAA's Ocean Service, Office for Coastal Management (OCM)

citeinfo.othercit: This classification is based on GIS and remote sensing data sets with variable ranges from the 1977 to 2010.

descript.abstract:
The probability rating which covers landcover mapping provides a continuum of wetness from dry to water. The layer is not a wetland classification but provides the wetland likelihood at a specific location. The rating was developed through a modelling process combining multiple GIS and remote sensing data sets including soil characteristics, elevation, existing wetland inventories, hydrographical extents and satellite imagery .
Data available via download from the Digital Coast, either from customizable download in NOAA's Digital Access Viewer or FTP download by regions.
descript.purpose: To inform likelihood of wetland and assess areas for potential mitigation or restoration.

timeperd.current: Range of dates of input data sets.

theme.themekey: EARTH SCIENCE : Land Surface : Land Use/Land Cover : Land Cover
EARTH SCIENCE : Land Surface : Land Use/Land Cover : Land Use Classes
EARTH SCIENCE : Human Dimensions : Environmental Governance/Management : Land Management : Land Use/Land Cover Classification

idinfo.useconst: Data set is not for use in litigation. While efforts have been made to ensure that these data are accurate and reliable, NOAA, cannot assume liability for any damages, or misrepresentations, caused by any inaccuracies in the data. NOAA makes no warranty, expressed or implied, nor does the fact of distribution constitute such a warranty.

attracc.attraccr: No accuracy assessment was performed on the wetland potential products.

dataqual.logic: Tests for logical consistency indicate that all row and column positions in the selected latitude/longitude window contain data. Conversion and integration with vector files indicates that all positions are consistent with earth coordinates covering the same area. Attribute files are logically consistent.
dataqual.complete: All pixels have been classified as designated by the model parameters.

horizpa.horizpar: Landsat scenes were geo-referenced by Eros Data Center.

vertacc.vertaccr: There was no terrain correction in the geo-referencing procedure. Input data were not altered before pre-processing.

citeinfo.origin: Department of Commerce (DOC), National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA), National Ocean Service (NOS), Office for Coastal Management (OCM)
citeinfo.title: NOAA Coastal Change Analysis Program (C-CAP) National Wetland Probability

srcinfo.srccitea: NOAA National Ocean Service, Office for Coastal Management (OCM)
srcinfo.srccontr: Wetland Potential Layers were used to find the likelihood of wetland potential and assess areas for potential mitigation or restoration.

procstep.procdesc: Data Collection: This involved the acquisition of pre-existing data sets to be used in the creation of the probability rating. These data were all sourced from national geospatial programs and most were available at no charge. The input data sets and their sources are: National Wetlands Inventory (NWI) from US Fisheries and Wildlife, National Hydrology Data set (NHD) from USGS, Soil Survey Geographic Database (SSURGO) from Natural Resource Conservation Service, 30m National Elevation Data set (NED) from USGS, and satellite imagery (Landsat, but 2009 NAIP imagery for state of Washington). Where available, wetland maps from County GIS Departments were used for validation.

cntperp.cntorg: NOAA Office for Coastal Management Coastal Change Analysis Program (C-CAP)

procstep.procdesc:
Pre-process data: Each data sets mentioned above was preprocessed in preparation for the comprehensive modelling step (see the last process step). This included converting vector data (NWI and NHD areas & flowlines) into the ERDAS IMAGINE raster format and resampling to a 30m spatial resolution. The SSURGO data was processed to create a percent hydric soils layer ranging from 0-100. Additionally, a slope and curvature models were derived from the 30m NED. Cloud free Landsat 7 TM scenes were acquired for each regional project.
The Hydric Soils and NHD raster were then modelled based on topographic characteristics to remove areas that did not support wetlands such as areas with high slopes and convexity. The NWI rasterized polygons were recoded to give each class a rating with a higher rating designated to the Woody wetland classes. The resulting raster layers from these preprocesing steps provided the building blocks for the probability rating.

cntorgp.cntorg: NOAA Office for Coastal Management Coastal Change Analysis Program (C-CAP)

cntinfo.hours: Monday to Friday, 8 a.m. to 5 p.m., Eastern Standard Time

procstep.procdesc: The final step was to generate a comprehensive probability rating that combined information from the various modelled layers. Multiple models were constructed that weighted the NWI rasterized polygons and linear data, Topo modelled Soils, Topo modelled NHD Polygons and Topo modelled image segments that intersected with NHD flowlines. No single model was developed to combine all of the processed data; instead, many models were developed that contributed to the final rating. As a result, based on the combination of: inclusion in the NWI, percentage of hydric soils and location in the floodplain a layer was generated with values ranging from 0 (Unlikely) to 8 (highly likely). Areas of open water were designated as 10.

cntorgp.cntorg: NOAA Office for Coastal Management Coastal Change Analysis Program (C-CAP)

enttyp.enttypd: Processed by regions, delineated by similar geographic characteristics, scene boundaries, hydrological units, and county boundaries. Regions: U.S. West Coast, U.S. Great Lakes, U.S. New England, U.S. Mid-Atlantic, U.S. Southeast, and U.S. Gulf of Mexico.

attr.attrdef: Likelihood of wetland as determined by NOAA Coastal Change Analysis Program (C-CAP)

edom.edomvd: The value indicates there is an extremely low likelihood of wetness. A value of 10 is designated for open water.
The value indicates a likelihood of wetness, where 0 is very unlikely and 8 is highly likely.
The value indicates a likelihood of wetness, where 0 is very unlikely and 8 is highly likely.
The value indicates a likelihood of wetness, where 0 is very unlikely and 8 is highly likely.
The value indicates a likelihood of wetness, where 0 is very unlikely and 8 is highly likely.
The value indicates a likelihood of wetness, where 0 is very unlikely and 8 is highly likely.
The value indicates a likelihood of wetness, where 0 is very unlikely and 8 is highly likely.
The value indicates a likelihood of wetness, where 0 is very unlikely and 8 is highly likely.
The value indicates a likelihood of wetness, where 0 is very unlikely and 8 is highly likely.

distinfo.distliab: Users must assume responsibility to determine the appropriate use of these data. DATA SHOULD NOT BE USED FOR LEGALLY BINDING APPLICATIONS.
distinfo.custom: This data can be obtained on-line at the following URL: http://coast.noaa.gov/dataviewer/index.html?action=advsearch&qType=in&qFld=projectid&qVal=4708
